target: SEC-8b 8b.7 closing-commitment prose softening — replace two self-attestation sentences with we-commit framing per curmudgeon SEC-8b.c1 Hole 2 (moderate) Recommendation A. Addresses ISS-1191.
scope_summary: Two single-sentence prose patches inside data/sections.json part8b.html — both within 8b.7 'Closing Commitment' detail prose. Curmudgeon SEC-8b.c1 Hole 2 (moderate) flagged the section's totalizing self-claim ('already in force across this review') as unverified self-attestation contradicted by spot-checks of pre-2026-04-18 citations that lack 'as of [date]' labels, and the 'doing more work than a review that cites only the latest version' sentence as self-laudatory framing. Recommendation A: soften both to we-commit framing and file an audit issue for legacy citation compliance. This EXP implements Recommendation A (the lower-cost option vs. Recommendation B's full pre-ship audit). No verdict change on any WIN; this is methodological hygiene on the Part 8b section's self-presentation.
kernel_of_truth: The replacement-standard commitment IS genuinely in force for content authored from 2026-04-18 forward — recent EXPs (e.g. EXP-302, EXP-358, EXP-395) consistently carry dated citations in the 'as of [date]' / 'checked_at' / 'fetched [date]' patterns, and the change-log preservation rule has been honored (closed-issues.json, expansion-tracker.json, monitor/changes/ all retained). The dome author's Point 5 critique does land a real distinction — pre-2026-04-18 prose was authored under looser citation discipline. The fix is not to retract the commitment but to scope it honestly (forward-from-integration-date) and file the legacy audit as a tracked follow-up rather than asserting compliance has been done.

patches.target_locator: part8b.html — 8b.7 Closing Commitment, paragraph 1 (the 'fair-auditor list is a good-faith move' paragraph), final sentence ('A review that cites with dates ...').
patches.current_text: The rejection is not defensive — the replacement standard holds us to a higher bar. A review that cites with dates, engages current content, and preserves the trajectory is doing more work than a review that cites only the latest version.
patches.proposed_text: The rejection is not defensive — the replacement standard holds us to a higher bar than "only current wording," because it ties accountability to the dated record. Whether our prose consistently meets that bar is an empirical question, not a self-attestation; the audit hooks in paragraph 2 are what would make the answer checkable.
patches.rationale: Curmudgeon SEC-8b.c1 Hole 2 (moderate). The 'doing more work than a review that cites only the latest version' framing is a comparison-with-other-reviews self-praise that the curmudgeon flagged as part of the totalizing self-claim pattern (Recommendation A: 'Soften ... replace ... with We commit to this standard going forward'). The replacement preserves the substantive content (the replacement standard is stronger than 'only current wording', and the strengthening comes from tying accountability to the dated record) while removing the self-comparison framing. The 'empirical question, not a self-attestation' clause explicitly forecloses the totalizing reading and points the reader at the audit hooks in the next paragraph — making the section's claim falsifiable rather than self-celebratory.
patches.regression_risk: Low. The argumentative thrust is unchanged: rejection of 'only current wording' in favor of dated-citation + always-current-engagement + preserved-change-log is preserved. The 'higher bar' framing is preserved. Only the rhetorical posture changes from self-comparison to self-empirical-claim.

patches.target_locator: part8b.html — 8b.7 Closing Commitment, paragraph 2 (the 'Integration schedule' paragraph), final sentence ('The replacement-standard commitment (8b.5) is already in force across this review; this section makes it explicit.').
patches.current_text: The replacement-standard commitment (8b.5) is already in force across this review; this section makes it explicit.
patches.proposed_text: The replacement-standard commitment (8b.5) is in force for citations and predictions registered from 2026-04-18 forward (the integration date of this fair-auditor list; new EXPs carry "as of [date]" / "checked_at" / "fetched [date]" labels by convention). A retrospective audit of pre-2026-04-18 citations — to bring legacy references into the same compliance and flag any that conflict with currently-served dome content — is tracked as a follow-up open issue.
patches.rationale: Curmudgeon SEC-8b.c1 Hole 2 (moderate) Recommendation A core fix. The current sentence ('already in force across this review') is the totalizing self-claim flagged as unverified: spot-checks of Section 4.1 EXP-182 update and Section 2b.5 apr18 update show citations to dome content without explicit 'as of [date]' tags. The replacement scopes the in-force claim to the temporally honest range (2026-04-18 forward, the integration date of this list) and acknowledges the pre-2026-04-18 audit as a tracked follow-up. The parenthetical lists the actual citation conventions in use post-integration ('as of [date]' / 'checked_at' / 'fetched [date]') so a reader can verify the convention exists rather than taking the claim on faith. The audit ISS will be filed by the decider as part of integrating this EXP (proposal-ISS-1191-resolution requests it).
patches.regression_risk: Low. The 'in force' framing is preserved but scoped. The legacy-audit commitment adds a tracked deliverable rather than removing one. The substantive position of the section (Point 5 rejected with stronger replacement) is unchanged. The cross-reference to 8b.5 is preserved.

proposed_follow_up_issue.title: Pre-2026-04-18 citation-compliance audit — bring legacy references into 'as of [date]' format
proposed_follow_up_issue.description: Per SEC-8b.c1 Hole 2 (moderate) Recommendation A and EXP-399 PATCH-399-B: spot-checks of pre-2026-04-18 content (Section 4.1 EXP-182 update, Section 2b.5 apr18 update) show citations to dome content without explicit 'as of [date]' tags — only URLs. The 8b.7 closing commitment now scopes the in-force claim to 2026-04-18 forward and flags this audit as the tracked legacy-compliance follow-up. Scope: walk data/sections.json prose for citations to john09289.github.io URLs or related dome surfaces lacking explicit date labels, list non-compliant instances, and either (a) add 'as of [date]' labels using poller-archive timestamps where date can be inferred, or (b) accept and document the labelled exception. Output: a citation-compliance report (count of references audited, count compliant, count updated, count documented as exception) plus the per-section patches. Bounded scope — only data/sections.json prose, not WINs or predictions (those have separate audit hooks via field-level conventions).
proposed_follow_up_issue.routing_hint: PATCH or ROUTE-TO-ANALYST per decider matrix. Bounded scope (single file, audit-then-patch), expected output is single citation-compliance report + per-section patches.
proposed_follow_up_issue.note_for_decider: Please file this ISS and substitute its ID into PATCH-399-B's proposed_text in place of 'a follow-up open issue' so the cross-reference is concrete. Suggested substitution: replace 'tracked as a follow-up open issue.' with 'tracked as ISS-NNNN.' where NNNN is the filed issue's ID.

anticipated_objections.objection: The original 'already in force across this review' is defensible if 'in force' is read as forward-looking ('this standard governs all future content') rather than retrospective ('all existing prose already meets it'). The curmudgeon's complaint about retroactive compliance is a category confusion — methodological standards typically apply prospectively from their statement date.
anticipated_objections.response: Two responses. (1) The original text says 'across this review' which is the totalizing reading; if forward-only was intended, the prose should have said so. The patched text preserves the forward-looking commitment ('in force for citations and predictions registered from 2026-04-18 forward') while removing the totalizing ambiguity. (2) Even on the most charitable reading, a section that announces a citation standard immediately invites the question 'and how does your existing prose hold up to it?' — the patched text answers that question (audit tracked) rather than dismissing it (asserting compliance without verification). Either way the section becomes more honest, not less.

anticipated_objections.objection: Filing an audit ISS for pre-2026-04-18 citations may produce more noise than signal. Most pre-2026-04-18 prose was authored to journalistic citation standards that include URLs and date context even when not in the canonical 'as of [date]' format. The audit may close with zero actionable patches.
anticipated_objections.response: An audit that closes with 'spot-check found N references, M already substantially compliant, K updated, all addressed' is a clean record and operationally proves the methodological commitment. An unverified self-claim ('already in force across this review') invites repeat curmudgeon flagging on every closing-commitment-style claim we make. The tracked-audit version is more durable even if the audit itself surfaces little. Operating cost is bounded — single file (data/sections.json), single citation type (john09289.github.io URLs), expected one decider session to triage.

anticipated_objections.objection: Why not just delete the entire 'is already in force / this section makes it explicit' framing without adding a replacement? The 8b.5 (d) replacement standard is published; the reader can see the commitment is stated. The 8b.7 closing meta-comment is rhetorical filler.
anticipated_objections.response: The 8b.7 paragraph 2 has structural purpose — it tells the reader how the various adoptions ship (immediate vs. follow-up expansions). The closing sentence currently anchors the section's commitment to the replacement standard within that schedule (when does Point 5's commitment take effect? — answer: it's already in force). Deleting without replacing would leave the schedule paragraph asymmetric (4 items have ship-timing, the 5th doesn't). The patched sentence preserves the structural completeness ('Point 5's commitment is in force from 2026-04-18 forward; the legacy audit is tracked separately') while removing the unverified self-claim. The structural fit is the reason for replacement-over-deletion.

tldr_review: Existing 8b.7 TLDR reads 'We commit to dated citations, always-current engagement, and a preserved change log as a symmetric rule — one that applies to us at the same strength we expect to apply to any site under audit. If we fall short, the instance should be named and we will fix it.' This is already the commit-framing the curmudgeon recommends; it does not contain the 'already in force' self-claim. The 'If we fall short' clause is exactly the empirical-question framing being patched into paragraph 2. No TLDR update needed. The 8b.5 TLDR is also fine — it states the standard ('we always engage current wording and we preserve the dated record') as our application of the standard, not as a totalizing self-claim about historical compliance, and the curmudgeon's hole was scoped to 8b.7 specifically.
test_impact: None expected. data/sections.json string-content edit on part8b.html field, two single-sentence replacements. tests/run.sh should pass without modification — there are no numerical claims or layer/rate values touched.
integration_instructions: 1) Apply PATCH-399-A and PATCH-399-B to data/sections.json part8b.html (find_pattern / replace_pattern style). 2) File the proposed_follow_up_issue as ISS-NNNN (analyst-flagged: this is the audit deliverable referenced in PATCH-399-B). 3) Substitute the new ISS-NNNN ID into PATCH-399-B's proposed_text — replace 'tracked as a follow-up open issue.' with 'tracked as ISS-NNNN.' so the cross-reference resolves. 4) Mark ISS-1191 status=fixed-pending-verification with fix_details referencing EXP-399. 5) Rebuild HTML (node build.js html) and run tests/run.sh.
notes_for_curmudgeon: Curmudgeon SEC-8b.c1 verdict_holds was true with confidence 'high'; only Hole 2 (moderate) had Recommendation A as the soften-and-file option. This EXP implements that option exactly, with the two sentence-replacements specified and the audit ISS proposed for filing. No expansion to other Hole-flagged items (Hole 0 4.2-collision, Hole 1 .adoption-label CSS, Hole 4 µGal misattribution, Hole 6 calibration-formula softening, Hole 11 L3a non-discrimination promotion) — those are out of scope for ISS-1191 and tracked elsewhere.
